title: Anchor the audit chain head externally via git-exporter or OpenTimestamps before any cross-actor tamper-evident claim ships
notes: Substrate DONE + green: external audit-anchor primitive added to INTKB store â€” appendAnchor (append-only, hash-chained anchor log of the chain head) + verifyAnchors (cross-checks live chain vs anchored snapshots). Catches HISTORY_REWRITTEN â€” the silent full re-hash-forward rewrite that verifyAuditChain passes clean. PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#187 (5/5 tests, validate green). REMAINING to apply in the product: (1) merge #187; (2) rebuild the plugin bundle with the updated store; (3) wire govern.ts to appendAnchor after each govern + git-commit/push the anchor file (the external tamper-evidence); (4) add a verify path; (5) update the umbrella README 'what the receipt does NOT do' box â€” the anchor now EXISTS (no longer 'on the roadmap').
close_reason: DONE end-to-end: anchor primitive merged to INTKB (#187); plugin govern.ts commits the chain head to a git-backed append-only anchor log per pass; brain_audit_verify (verifyAnchors) detects chain breaks AND silent full rewrites; README honesty box updated (implemented, not roadmap); republished governed-second-brain@0.1.3 with npm provenance. Verified: govern anchored{committed:true}+git commit, verify clean on intact brain, tamperâ†’ok=false (ANCHOR_HASH_MISMATCH+HISTORY_REWRITTEN).

title: Reframe the brain and brain-save skills for public single-user use and align allowed-tools with the real MCP tool surface

title: Build the public local stdio MCP server that drives qmd, the govern kernel, and ~/.teamkb in-process, exposing read (search) and write (capture, govern, promote)
close_reason: Built src/local-server.ts + src/govern.ts: self-contained in-process stdio MCP server (brain_search/status read; brain_capture/govern/transition write). brain_govern hand-wires ingestâ†’Curator.processBatchâ†’runExportâ†’qmd index, daemon-free. Smoke-proven end-to-end (751bbe5).

title: Upgrade brain_search to qmd hybrid query (auto-expansion + rerank) plus an embed step in govern, for natural-language recall
description: brain_search currently runs qmd search (BM25, AND-semantics) which is brittle for natural-language queries: hyphenated/compound terms (e.g. 'in-process') tokenize to zero matches and fail the AND, so verbose questions often return nothing. qmd query (hybrid: auto-expansion + vector + LLM rerank) handles NL far better but needs qmd embed (a one-time ~333MB model download + embedding pass). Decide whether to add an embed step to brain_govern and switch brain_search to qmd query. Shared improvement (the internal brain uses the same BM25 adapter).
notes: Empirical confirmation (this session): qmd hybrid 'query' with the full NL question 'does the brain run in-process without a daemon' â†’ 1 hit, while BM25 'search in-process' â†’ 0. The upgrade path (switch brain_search to qmd query + add an embed step in govern) fixes the natural-language recall gap. Cost: ~333MB embedding model + one embed pass per govern.

title: Add gsb.lock.json reproducible pin and a CI smoke that runs the full chain against the pinned set
Harden installer security: npm provenance and checksums, pin qmd and ICO by SHA or tag, mint a local token, drop curl-bash
notes: Basic npm publish DONE: governed-second-brain@0.1.1 live (npx governed-second-brain init verified from the registry end-to-end). Caught+fixed a packaging bug (0.1.0 baked a linux-only better-sqlite3 via a files glob â†’ 4.2MB; unpublished; 0.1.1 ships only the .cjs, 249KB, native dep installs per-platform). Installer pins ICO @^1.14.0. REMAINING for this bead: npm provenance (needs a CI release workflow w/ OIDC, like ICO's release.yml), 2FA/OTP on publish, checksums, and the local-token mint.
close_reason: Installer/publish hardening done: (1) npm PROVENANCE via a CI release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml, OIDC id-token, tag-version guard) â€” governed-second-brain@0.1.2 carries a SLSA provenance attestation + registry signature; (2) ICO pinned @^1.14.0; (3) qmd version-check (>=2.x); (4) runtime fully bundled (ajv inlined, only better-sqlite3 external); (5) install is npx, not curl|bash; (6) loud pre-flight consent. N/A for the single-trust-domain local stdio model: local-token mint + origin/host-header check (those are for the future hosted tier). Reproducible pin (gsb.lock.json) tracked separately in qy7.10.

title: Build the one-command npx installer that builds the local brain over a folder, idempotent with --resume and --clean
notes:
Phase B milestone (e1b5104, plugin repo): bin/init.mjs 'init <folder> --index-only' works end-to-end â€” consent pre-flight, vendors better-sqlite3, drives the bundled MCP server to capture each file â†’ govern â†’ qmd index. Verified: 3 captured â†’ 3 promoted, searchable with qmd:// citations, audit chain verifies (3 events, 0 breaks), ZERO egress. --clean + additive-default idempotency. Remaining for this bead/Phase B: full ICO-compile mode (opt-in egress, qy7.6), automatic Claude Code/Cowork MCP registration, and npx publish + provenance (qy7.9).
Added automatic Claude Code MCP registration (e663889): installer runs 'claude mcp add governed-brain' (idempotent, --scope user default, --no-register opt-out) pointing at the vendored runtime + brain env; falls back to printing the command if claude CLI absent. Verified at project scope (writes correct .mcp.json, claude mcp list sees it, global config untouched). So 'init --index-only' is now a true one-command experience for the local/no-egress path: build brain â†’ register â†’ use. Remaining: full ICO-compile mode (egress), Cowork registration, npx publish+provenance (qy7.9).
Full ICO-compile mode wired + verified (plugin 705e686). 'init <folder>' (no --index-only) runs ico initâ†’ingestâ†’compile allâ†’spoolâ†’brain_govern; uses DeepSeek (ICO_PROVIDER=deepseek), NOT Anthropic credits. Verified: 2-file corpus â†’ 6 passes â†’ 10 derived memories promoted â†’ searchable + audited (11 events, 0 breaks). Installer now covers: index-only (zero-egress), full-compile (DeepSeek), native-dep vendoring, auto Claude Code MCP registration. Remaining: npx publish+provenance (qy7.9), Cowork registration.

close_reason: Resolved: ship the prebuilt esbuild bundle that inlines the 8 INTKB workspace packages (INTKB never published, stays private). ICO consumed via npx intentional-cognition-os@^1.14.0; only better-sqlite3 installs per-platform. Plugin published as governed-second-brain on npm.

title: Add an index-only no-egress mode and a loud pre-flight egress consent for the ICO compile step
close_reason: Both done: --index-only no-egress mode (captureâ†’governâ†’index, zero network) + a loud pre-flight egress consent on the full-compile path. Verified end-to-end.

title: Productize Governed Second Brain into a public local-first plugin, installer, and marketplace listing
description: Umbrella epic for turning the Compile-Then-Govern stack into a public, local-first Governed Second Brain that outsiders (esp. the Cowork community) can install and build their own governed, qmd://-cited brain with a tamper-evident audit trail, from inside Claude Code or Cowork. Internal brain + team mode stay untouched. Phases: A public local-MCP plugin (read+write) Â· B npx installer (egress consent + --index-only) Â· C marketplace distribution Â· D cross-link/thesis-fold/tracking. Plus standalone MUST-FIX beads (audit-anchor HIGH, egress mode, installer security, gsb.lock.json).
notes:
GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#1 â€” https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/1. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out â€” `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-qy7` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-qy7` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
Phase A landed (751bbe5): the public local-first MCP plugin is built and gate-green. Read+write tools (brain_search/status, brain_capture/govern/transition) run in-process, daemon-free; captureâ†’governâ†’promoteâ†’search verified end-to-end with a qmd:// citation and a verified SHA-256 hash-chained audit (1 event, 0 breaks). Both skills pass marketplace 8-field tier (Grade B); the original dead-tool gap is closed (allowed-tools match the 5 registered tools). Beads qy7.1â€“qy7.4 closed. Known follow-up filed: natural-language retrieval (qmd hybrid query + embed). Next: Phase B installer.
Repo restructure (per ownership decision): the public PLUGIN moved to a new personal repo jeremylongshore/governed-second-brain-plugin (smoke-green in its new home). intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain reverts to umbrella/landing-only and points at the plugin + engines. Engines (ICO, INTKB) stay personal. Company org = umbrella + private internal catalog. Program beads + this issue stay on the umbrella as the program tracker. Phase B installer will live in the plugin repo.
DeepSeek wired into ICO (engine) to power full-compile without Anthropic credits â€” PR jeremylongshore/intentional-cognition-os#137 MERGED (provider switch ICO_PROVIDER=deepseek, default anthropic unchanged; default path verified byte-identical, 31 regression tests + CI green). Installer full-compile now runs on DeepSeek end-to-end against local ICO. Internal team compile UNAFFECTED (additive, opt-in; the intent-brain plugin doesn't run compile).
Distribution live: ICO auto-released v1.14.0 with DeepSeek (npx intentional-cognition-os@1.14.0); plugin published governed-second-brain@0.1.1 â€” 'npx governed-second-brain init <folder>' works end-to-end from npm (both --index-only and full-compile-on-DeepSeek). qy7.7 closed (bundle, not publish-INTKB). qy7.9 remaining: provenance/2FA/checksums.
Security hardening landed: governed-second-brain@0.1.2 published with npm PROVENANCE (SLSA attestation via CI OIDC release workflow). Also verified this turn: INTKB repo builds clean (only the flagged DECR license edit in its tree); the live ~/.teamkb brain is untouched (680 memories, last modified Jun 14). qy7.7 + qy7.9 closed. Remaining: gsb.lock.json reproducible pin + CI full-chain smoke (qy7.10), Cowork registration, audit-anchor HIGH (qy7.5), cross-link/docs (qy7.12).
